items.descriptionHtml:
A career that’s the whole package!

At Conga, we’ve built a community where our colleagues can thrive. Here you’ll find opportunities to innovate and support growth through individual and team development, all within an environment where every voice is heard.

Conga lines up commercial operations so companies run as connected, smarter businesses. By unifying the people and processes that drive commerce, Conga aligns pricing, quoting, contracting, rebates, and communications so teams stay in sync and buyers keep moving forward. The result is trusted decisions, consistent buyer experiences, and profitable growth. More than 10,000 customers worldwide, including over 50% of the Fortune 100®, trust Conga when commercial complexity is high and global impact is on the line.

Our approach is grounded in the Conga Way, a framework that reflects our values and drives everything from hiring to decision-making, as well as key programs including recognition. Created with direct input from our colleagues, the Conga Way forms the foundation of our vibrant culture.

Job Title: Principal Product Manager

Locations: Bangalore

Reports to: Director, Product Management

A quick snapshot…

The Principal Product Manager at Conga is responsible for the product direction, planning, execution, and launch of product releases. Conga thrives in solving our customer’s business problems and creating raving fans in the process. Here you bridge the technical and business worlds as you design products that our customers and their customers love! Join the team at Conga to help drive the next phase of growth.

Why it’s a big deal…

A Conga Principal Product Manager is continuously adapting & is excited to work on Products, cross-functionally to guide products from conception to launch by connecting the technical and business worlds. You have a technical passion to break down complex problems into steps that drive the product development lifecycle including analyzing, positioning, packaging, promoting & tailoring our solutions to meet our customer needs.

Are you the person we’re looking for?

Relevant Experience. You should have 12+ years of overall experience and 3-5 years in building B2B SAS products from concept to post-launch, API, and integrations & familiarity with building ML based products. Ability and confidence to work closely with select customers in pre-sales and collaborative scenarios. Experience with integrated solutions for major platforms such as Microsoft, SAP, Oracle, or Salesforce is strongly desired. You’ll be involved in technical publications, providing guidance to product education teams for technical and product documentation, and transitioning finished projects to the Customer-facing teams.

Research and Creativity. Own the research, action plans, adoption plans, and results for the products. Map business ideas and use cases into product requirements and roadmap, set goals, determine priorities, and track deliverables. You will create solutions to problems with our products while always focusing on the best user experience and balancing company goals, product cohesion, and ease of implementation, research, wireframing, and incorporating user feedback into the product.

Leadership skills. Work across multiple teams to deliver quality software at a consistent pace, which aligns to the product vision. Guide the scrum teams to deliver great software at an impressive pace. Keep the team aligned with the work at hand and the completion of the greater feature, gather product requirements from stakeholders, manage release status for development and communicate status to other departments in the company. Work closely with the development team to decompose high-level features into user stories, quickly provide just-in-time decisions to system-level questions, and defect triage.

Analyze and prioritize. Schedule development work for releases. Work with product management and development teams to create and maintain product priorities, track schedules against delivery commitments, and escalate risks and issues. Work with and maintain communication with stakeholders. Analyze product metrics to understand product performance.

Customer experience. Aligning customer experience strategies with marketing initiatives and informing customers about new product features and functionalities. Analyzing customer feedback on product ranges and new releases and preparing reports accordingly. Documenting processes and logging technical issues, as well as customer compliments and complaints.

Education: A Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or equivalent

Here’s What Will Give You An Edge…

 Champion the Customer. You understand that customers are one of the business’s most important assets and you take that seriously. With more than 11,000 customers around the globe entrusting Conga with their business we make a commitment to their success and would love to hear how you will too!

Natural collaborator. You work effectively with Product Manager, are proficient with Agile and a collaborative cross-functional approach to building awesome software. You are comfortable working with teams and collaborating on best practices across multiple Agile teams. You constantly seek opinions and solicit feedback to create the best work possible. You don’t know any other way. It’s a team effort and you completely appreciate that.

Passion. We know it when we see it. Passion is not about saying how much you love what you do in your most excited voice. Passion is revealed in your truest self. It’s about what you’ve accomplished; how you want to grow; the ideas you have; your philosophies. It’s demonstrating through your words and your actions that you truly believe in what you do -- and where you work. That it matters to you. And that’s pretty cool. 

Did we pique your interest? 

If this sounds like the kind of job you would love in the kind of environment where you would thrive, please click apply. We'd love to hear from you! 

Preferred Resume Format

We accept resumes in any format, we suggest using PDF or plain text. These formats help ensure that your resume’s formatting remains intact, making it easier for our recruiters to review your application promptly.

Don’t meet every requirement for the role?  

Studies have shown that women and members of ethnic minorities are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At Conga we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You just might be the right candidate for this or other roles. 

Additional Information

Conga is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, age, national origin, disability, parental or pregnancy status, marriage and civil partnership, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We understand interviewing can be stressful for those with disabilities. If reasonable accommodation is needed to allow you to show us your best self, please let your recruiter know as soon as possible. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines. Conga is not open to third party solicitation or resumes for our posted FTE positions. Resumes received from third party agencies that are unsolicited will be considered complimentary.

Conga's Applicant Privacy Statement

The information you provide during the application process will be used in accordance with Conga’s Applicant Privacy Statement (https://conga.com/applicant-privacy-statement). By submitting your application, you acknowledge and agree to the terms outlined in this privacy statement.

items.descriptionHtml:
Principal Product Manager

Bangalore/Hyderabad/Pune (Hybrid)

12+yrs Experience; Preferably with Cybersecurity/Data Security Products



What You’ll Do

In this role, you will serve as the Principal Product Manager driving Teradata’s data security product management strategy and execution across our global portfolio. You will define and influence how Teradata protects the world’s most sensitive enterprise data; spanning data governance and compliance, identity and access control, AI Agent security among others.

Your impact will be shaping how customers safely harness Petabyte-scale analytics and AI through fine-grained access controls and end-to-end encryption and delivering a secure and trusted environment to run their mission critical analytics. You will accelerate the delivery of Enterprise security roadmap balancing innovation with performance, governance, and compliance.

Success in this role will be measured by delivering enterprise-grade data security solutions for hybrid and multi-cloud deployments, differentiating Teradata and unlocking measurable customer value while preserving scalability, reliability, and cost-efficiency.


Who you will work with

On our team, we collaborate with engineering, cloud infrastructure, security operations, governance, CISO organization and UX functions to bring next-generation data security capabilities into Teradata’s enterprise ecosystem. We are responsible for the product vision, roadmap, and execution of data security initiatives, ensuring they meet both business and technical objectives.

This role reports to the Senior Director of Product Management and will partner closely with product engineering and platform leaders worldwide. You will act as the strategic data security product leader for Teradata, influencing global product direction.


What makes you a qualified candidate

  • Bachelor's or master's degree in computer science, Cybersecurity, Information Security, Engineering, or related field.
  • 12+ years of product management experience, including 6+ years leading data security, data governance, or cybersecurity products at enterprise scale.
  • Deep technical expertise across the data security stack, including data classification and lineage, fine-grained access control (RBAC/ABAC, row- and column-level security), encryption at rest and in transit, key management (KMS, BYOK/HYOK), tokenization, and secrets management.
  • Proven success embedding security and governance capabilities into enterprise data platforms, analytics, or cloud environments—ideally at petabyte scale.
  • Strong technical fluency with identity and access management ecosystems and integration with enterprise IdPs.
  • Hands-on familiarity with threat detection, audit logging, anomaly detection, and data loss prevention for structured and unstructured data.
  • Demonstrated knowledge of hybrid and multi-cloud architectures and their impact on security posture, data residency, sovereignty, and cost optimization.
  • Deep expertise applying compliance and regulatory frameworks and emerging AI governance standards to product design and audit readiness.
  • Experience defining APIs, SDKs, policy languages, and developer integrations that make secure-by-default the easiest path for enterprise builders.
  • Exceptional communication skills with the ability to influence C-level stakeholders—including CISOs, CDOs, and CIOs—and articulate data security product strategy to both technical and executive audiences.
  • Track record of customer engagement, market analysis, and competitive benchmarking against the native security stacks of major cloud providers.


What you will bring

  • Expertise designing zero-trust data architectures across cloud and hybrid environments, ensuring least-privilege access without compromising analytics performance.
  • Hands-on knowledge of confidential computing, hardware-backed key management, and emerging privacy-enhancing technologies such as differential privacy and secure multi-party computation.
  • Ability to bridge governed, sensitive enterprise data with modern AI/ML and analytics workloads—including how to secure data used to train, fine-tune, and serve AI models.
  • A strong understanding of responsible data use—covering auditability, lineage, consent management, retention, and the handling of regulated data classes (PII, PHI, PCI).
  • Vision to anticipate industry trends—quantum-safe cryptography, AI-driven threat detection, data sovereignty mandates—and position Teradata’s data security offerings as differentiated and enterprise-ready.
  • Passion for enabling intuitive, secure-by-default user experiences (e.g., natural-language policy authoring, policy-as-code, automated risk insights, one-click compliance reporting).
  • Ability to operate as a senior leader—setting strategy, guiding technical direction, and influencing globally across product, engineering, GTM, and the security community.

items.descriptionHtml:
About Albertsons Companies Inc. (ACI):

As a leading food and drug retailer in the United States, Albertsons Companies, Inc. operates over 2,200 stores across 35 states and the District of Columbia. Our well-known banners across the United States, including Albertsons, Safeway, Vons, Jewel-Osco and others, serve more than 36 million U.S customers each week.

We build and shape technology solutions that solve customers’ problems every day, making things easier for them when they shop with us online or in a store. We have made bold, strategic moves to migrate and modernize our core foundational capabilities, positioning ourselves as the first fully cloud-based grocery tech company in the industry.

Our success is built on a one-team approach, driven by the desire to understand and enhance the customer experience. By constantly pushing the boundaries of retail, we are transforming shopping into an experience that is easy, efficient, fun and engaging.


About Albertsons India Capability Center:

At Albertsons India Capability Center, we're not just pushing the boundaries of technology and retail innovation, we're cultivating a space where ideas flourish and careers thrive. Our workplace in India is a vital extension of the Albertsons Companies Inc. workforce and important to the next phase in the company’s technology journey to support millions of customers’ lives every day.

At the Albertsons India Capability Center, we are raising the bar to grow across Technology & Engineering, AI, Digital and other company functions, and transform a 165-year-old American retailer. At Albertsons India Capability Center, associates collaborate directly with international teams, enhancing decision-making processes and organizational agility through exciting and pivotal projects. Your work will make history and help millions of lives each day come together around the joys of food and inspire their well-being.


Senior Product Manager:

Albertsons Companies is seeking a Sr. Product Manager to lead the vision, strategy, and execution of the Omni Partner Setup Platform—a foundational enterprise catalog system of record serving suppliers, merchants, buyers, and cross-functional teams across stores, e-com, 3rd party partners, supply chain, retail, and finance. This platform is central to Partner lifecycle management and we’re looking for a product leader who is passionate about transforming Partner introduction and setup through cutting-edge technologies, including Generative AI.


Roles & Responsibilities:

Experience Required / Skills Required:

  • Define, Prioritize and own the product roadmap for the Omni Partner Setup Platform, aligning with enterprise-wide goals and stakeholder needs.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams including engineering, UX, data, and business units to deliver seamless Partner setup experiences for suppliers, merchants, and buyers.
  • Translate complex business requirements into clear product features and user stories, ensuring alignment with e-com, stores, supply chain, retail, and finance workflows.
  • Champion the use of Generative AI to reimagine and simplify Partner setup workflows, improve data quality, and enhance user experience.
  • Drive platform scalability, data integrity, and automation across Partner onboarding and maintenance processes.
  • Partner with internal teams to ensure robust attribute mapping, governance, and compliance across systems.
  • Conduct user research, discovery sessions, and stakeholder interviews to inform product decisions and prioritize enhancements.
  • Define and track product success metrics, ensuring continuous improvement and business impact.
  • Support change management and rollout strategies for new capabilities across global teams.
  • 8 to 10 years of product management experience, preferably in enterprise platforms, master data management, supply chain, or retail systems.
  • 3 to 5 years of experience in Retail and change management with some relevant experience in Partner or Vendor management e.g. seller central, merchant portal, vendor portal etc.
  • 3 to 5 years in Product Management.
  • Proven experience in building and scaling internal tools or platforms with complex stakeholder ecosystems.
  • Good understanding of Partner lifecycle management, data governance, and enterprise system integrations.
  • Experience working in Agile environments with cross-functional global teams.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence across levels.
  • Must have: A strong sense of curiosity and a drive to explore emerging technologies and innovative solutions.
  • Growth Mindset, Product Mindset.
  • Customer insight – Data fluency, Voice of customer, working with UX.
  • Product Strategy – Outcomes orientation, Product Vision & Road mapping.
  • Product Execution – Feature Specs, High quality global agile delivery with own Engineering team at a product or retail company, not systems integration work for an existing product such as Magento, SAP or Salesforce.
  • Influencing People – Change Management, Cross functional partnerships for Discovery, UAT & Adoption.
  • Retail Experience.
  • Functional Experience – Partner or Vendor Management or Merchant Portal.
  • AI PM – for personal productivity across PM lifecycle and product builds.
  • Top Tier Undergrad or MBA.

Application and Interview Impersonation Notice:  Impersonating another individual when applying for employment, and/or participating in an interview process to assist another individual in obtaining employment, with Precisely Software Incorporated (“Precisely”) is unlawful.  If Precisely identifies such fraudulent conduct, then as applicable and to the extent permitted by law, the application will be rejected, an offer (if made) will be rescinded, or the employment will be terminated, and legal action may be taken against the impersonators.

 

Precisely is the leader in data integrity. We empower businesses to make more confident decisions based on trusted data through a unique combination of software, data enrichment products and strategic services. What does this mean to you? For starters, it means joining a company focused on delivering outstanding innovation and support that helps customers increase revenue, lower costs and reduce risk. In fact, Precisely powers better decisions for more than 12,000 global organizations, including 95 of the Fortune 100. Precisely's 2500 employees are unified by four company core values that are central to who we are and how we operate: Openness, Determination, Individuality, and Collaboration. We are committed to career development for our employees and offer opportunities for growth, learning and building community. With a "work from anywhere" culture, we celebrate diversity in a distributed environment with a presence in 30 countries as well as 20 offices in over 5 continents. Learn more about why it's an exciting time to join Precisely!

Overview:

Join our Data Integration team and take ownership of the Connect CDC product line. You will translate market trends and customer insights into a clear product roadmap. You will work closely with engineering, design, and go-to-market teams to deliver high-quality solutions. As the subject matter expert on Connect CDC, you will guide product development and identify growth opportunities. You will ensure the product continues to meet the evolving demands of our 12,000 customers worldwide. This is an individual contributor role with significant impact and visibility.

What you will do:

  • Define and own the Connect CDC roadmap, prioritizing capabilities that address customer needs and drive competitive differentiation in data integration and Change Data Capture.
  • Conduct ongoing research with customers, prospects, and analysts to surface unmet needs and inform product direction.
  • Author clear business requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria; maintain a well-prioritized backlog in close collaboration with engineering.
  • Partner with Engineering, UX, Support, and GTM teams throughout the product lifecycle to ensure on-time, high-quality delivery.
  • Monitor the CDC and data integration landscape and develop a working understanding of technical concepts such as connectors, pipelines, replication, and cloud/hybrid deployment.
  • Align product direction with internal stakeholders across Product, Sales, and Customer Success; serve as the go-to resource on Connect CDC capabilities and strategy.

What we are looking for:

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. A background in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field is helpful but not required.
  • Experience: 5–7 years of product management experience building SaaS platforms, shared services, or internal-facing capabilities used by multiple product teams.
  • Demonstrated ability to design and deliver reusable platform components—such as entitlement management, usage metering, tenant administration, and API-driven services—that support cross-team development at scale.
  • Proven ability to define platform standards, manage complex stakeholder groups, and drive execution in an Agile environment with multiple dependent teams.
  • Hands-on experience with Agile development practices and product tools such as Aha! or similar roadmapping and backlog management platforms.
  • Familiarity with data integration concepts such as CDC, ETL/ELT, connectors, or real-time data pipelines.
  • Travel is required: No.
  • Bonus points for: experience with hybrid cloud deployments (particularly AWS and MongoDB), hands-on familiarity with Kubernetes or API integration frameworks, exposure to CDC technologies, background in data quality or data governance, or familiarity with SQL, databases, or streaming platforms such as Kafka.

The personal data that you provide as a part of this job application will be handled in accordance with relevant laws. For more information about how Precisely handles the personal data of job applicants, please see the Precisely Candidate Privacy Notice

items.descriptionHtml:
Position: Principal Product Manager – Advertising

Business Unit: Customer Experience Orchestration

Product: Adobe Advertising

Location: Bengaluru

Opportunity

Adobe Advertising is the premier Enterprise platform for advertisers on the world’s largest publisher networks managing billions of dollars in advertising spend.

Brands are grappling with increasing complexity of media plans, rising media costs, changing consumer behavior and measurement challenges – while needing to do more with less. They are looking for incremental performance, enhanced governance, and to maximize the use of their brand data.

Working closely with the world’s top brands and agencies, you will bring a principled approach to surfacing self-service capabilities for advertising such as reporting, alerts, insights, recommendations, experiments, and actions. You will thoughtfully leverage cutting-edge advances in marketing science and artificial intelligence to build industry-first solutions addressing unmet and emerging needs.

For more details on Adobe Advertising, visit: https://www.adobe.com/advertising

What You Will Do

  • Deliver on complex roadmaps and ambitious bets working closely with customer success, design, data science, engineering, and program management by building delightful, reliable product experiences.
  • Deep dive into the APIs and capabilities of major advertising platforms to identify opportunities for incremental performance and enhanced governance.
  • Be hands-on with prototypes and data storyboards to supplement product requirements across discovery, definition, cross-functional alignment, development, distribution, and monetization
  • Ensure adoption of features by partnering with customer success, enablement, and sales.
  • Monitoring and analyzing product usage data to inform improvements
  • Regularly communicate learning, progress, and plans across org: horizontal and vertical

What You Need To Succeed

  • Deep Experience in Digital Advertising, plus 8+ years in top-tier Product Management
  • Exceptionally organized, detail-oriented, and execution mastery: finish projects cleanly
  • Clarity in messaging, speech, documents, and slides at varying technical and detail levels
  • Low ego and growth mindset: support and challenge team being honest, direct, and ethical
  • Deliver high quality artifacts and product with minimal guidance: high trust, high autonomy
  • Align global, cross-functional teams by pro-active communication, visibility, and inclusion
  • Hands-on Do-It-Yourself skills: handy with SQL, Python/R, building detailed prototypes
  • Master’s in Business; plus Computer Science degree and/or developer work experience

About Adobe

Adobe empowers everyone to create through innovative platforms and tools that unleash creativity, productivity and personalized customer experiences. Adobe’s industry-leading offerings including Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Firefly, Creative Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, and GenStudio enable people and businesses to turn ideas into impact, powered by AI and driven by human ingenuity.

Our 30,000+ employees worldwide are creating the future and raising the bar as we drive the next decade of growth. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. At Adobe, we believe that great ideas can come from anywhere in the organization. The next big idea could be yours.

Let’s Adobe together

At Adobe, we believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. Learn more about Adobe life, including our values and culture, focus on people, purpose and community, Adobe for All, comprehensive benefits programs, the stories we tell, the customers we serve, and how you can help us advance our mission of empowering everyone to create.

Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Learn more.

Adobe aims to make our Careers website and recruiting process accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call +1 408-536-3015.

AI Use Guidelines for Interviews:

Our interviews are designed to reflect your own skills and thinking. The use of AI or recording tools during live interviews is not permitted unless explicitly invited by the interviewer or approved in advance as part of a reasonable accommodation. If these tools are used inappropriately or in a way that misrepresents your work, your application may not move forward in the process.

At Adobe, we empower employees to innovate with AI — and we look for candidates eager to do the same. As part of the hiring experience, we provide clear guidance on where AI is encouraged during the process and where it’s restricted during live interviews. See how we think about AI in the hiring experience.

items.descriptionHtml:
Minimum qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
  • 8 years of experience in product management or related technical roles.
  • Experience taking technical products from conception to launch (e.g., ideation to execution, 0 to 1), specifically within video streaming, OTT platforms, or media infrastructure.
  • Experience with subscription business models (SVOD/TVOD), digital rights management, or business generation products.

Preferred qualifications:

  • Master's degree in a technology or business related field.
  • 5 years of experience working cross-functionally with engineering, UX/UI, sales finance, and other stakeholders.
  • 5 years of experience in a business function or role (e.g., marketing, business operations, consulting).
  • Experience working with cross-functional teams (e.g., Engineering, Legal, Business Development) to navigate regulatory or partner requirements.
  • Experience building self-serve platforms or enterprise tools that automate operational workflows (e.g., onboarding, agreement signing).
  • Knowledge of global media rights, music licensing, or geo-blocking technologies.

About the jobAt Google, we put our users first. The world is always changing, so we need Product Managers who are continuously adapting and excited to work on products that affect millions of people every day.

In this role, you will work cross-functionally to guide products from conception to launch by connecting the technical and business worlds. You can break down complex problems into steps that drive product development.

One of the many reasons Google consistently brings innovative, world-changing products to market is because of the collaborative work we do in Product Management. Our team works closely with creative engineers, designers, marketers, etc. to help design and develop technologies that improve access to the world's information. We're responsible for guiding products throughout the execution cycle, focusing specifically on analyzing, positioning, packaging, promoting, and tailoring our solutions to our users.

As a Senior Product Manager, you will help Primetime Studio democratize professional streaming on YouTube. You will transform our premium infrastructure into a scalable, self-serve platform, enabling niche sports, indie studios, and creators to launch Subscription-Supported Video on Demand (SVOD) and live channels with near-zero onboarding costs. You will bridge the gap between user content and premium media rights.At YouTube, we believe that everyone deserves to have a voice, and that the world is a better place when we listen, share, and build community through our stories. We work together to give everyone the power to share their story, explore what they love, and connect with one another in the process. Working at the intersection of cutting-edge technology and boundless creativity, we move at the speed of culture with a shared goal to show people the world. We explore new ideas, solve real problems, and have fun — and we do it all together.

Responsibilities

  • Execute a phased rollout strategy, moving from Live Ad-supported Video on Demand (AVOD) to SVOD to Transactional Video on Demand (TVOD) and global mass adoption.
  • Design automated onboarding flows eliminate operational friction, enabling click-to-accept standard terms and instant activation of Digital Rights Management (DRM), Virtual Private Network (VPN) detection, and geo-blocking.
  • Develop scalable tools for partners to create SVOD, TVOD rentals, season passes, and annual plans without manual engineering support.
  • Collaborate with engineering and legal to streamline critical barriers, specifically automating many tasks that are manual today and standardizing agreements and processes to support international scaling.


Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

items.descriptionHtml:
Job Title: Senior Product Manager


Company: Verse Innovation (Dailyhunt)

Location: Bangalore

WFO: Hybrid

Type: Full-Time


About Verse Innovation :

VerSe Innovation is the parent company of platforms like Dailyhunt, Josh, and since 2024, Magzter. The company is headquartered in Bengaluru and focuses on AI-powered local language content, digital media, creator ecosystem, and ad-tech solutions.


Job Description:

Verse Innovation is seeking a talented and motivated Senior Product Manager with 6-10 years of experience to join our dynamic and innovative product team. As an SPM at Verse Innovation, you will play a pivotal role in creating user-centric digital experiences that cater to our diverse & very large user base. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver engaging solutions that meet our users' needs. We are launching new agentic Ai products for the world, join us & be part of the revolution.


Responsibilities:

  • Define the product vision and strategy. This includes understanding the needs of the customer, identifying market opportunities, and developing a plan to bring the product to market.
  • Gather requirements and specifications. This involves working with stakeholders to understand their needs and translating them into technical requirements.
  • Design the product. Work with the design team to create wireframes, mockups, and prototypes to test and refine the product's design.
  • Develop the product. This involves working with engineers and designers to build the product according to the specifications.
  • Test the product. This involves running usability tests and beta tests to ensure that the product meets the needs of the customer.
  • Launch the product. This involves marketing and distributing the product to the target market.
  • Gather feedback and iterate. This involves collecting feedback from users and making changes to the product to improve it.


⠀Qualifications:

  • 6-10 years of experience in product management or a related role.
  • Good conceptual tech understanding of how android ios web apps & backend works
  • Strong understanding of product development, marketing, and data analysis.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to work cross-functionally with engineers, designers, and other stakeholders.
  • Ability to think strategically and solve problems.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

  • If you are a motivated Senior Product Manager with a passion for improving experiences and are excited about the opportunity to work on a product like Dailyhunt, we encourage you to apply and be part of our journey.

items.descriptionHtml:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.

Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.

Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.

Payments is one of the company’s major growth enablers. Putting customers at the heart of everything we do, we connect communities and empower our people to deliver exceptional commerce experiences to drive eBay’s long-term growth and market share. eBay seeks an experienced Product Manager to develop, optimize and scale the capabilities of eBay's Payments Platform.

This role involves tackling the challenges of evolving a robust marketplace payment solution into a scalable, multi-tenant "Payments-as-a-Service" platform. A critical challenge is to decouple our existing payment boundaries to build a "plug-and-play" suite of services — including embedded payments, compliance, and risk — that can be seamlessly adopted by any tenant, for example eBay subsidiaries. The Product Manager will lead the strategic evolution of the financial services, crafting future-proof interfaces and customizable design components that handle operational heavy-lifting while ensuring tenant isolation and independent scale.

What You Will Accomplish

  • Define and drive the product vision for the "Payments-as-a-Service" track, evolving our embedded payments capabilities for marketplaces as well as a full PayFac offering for subsidiaries and future clients.
  • Partner with cross-functional teams to design "fit-for-purpose" boundaries between the marketplace and payments layers, ensuring services like acquiring, payouts, financial reporting and ledgering are modular and scalable.
  • As the product owner, lead the development of both API-first services and "drop-in" UX components (SDKs) that allow marketplace as well as PayFac tenants to quickly onboard and ramp.
  • Analyze data and industry trends to identify optimization hypotheses for multi-tenant scaling, focusing on automating onboarding, compliance checks, and financial reconciliation for partners.
  • Clearly set the product/backlog prioritization with a balance of near-term outcomes (e.g., migrating subsidiary sellers to the new platform) and longer-term investments in platform future-proofing.
  • Build a case for your hypotheses, A/B test implementation strategies, and implement winners that reduce integration time and operational burden for our tenants.
  • Effectively communicate strategies & trade-offs all the way from senior leaders to the development team, ensuring alignment on the shift from internal tools to commercial-grade SaaS capabilities.


What You Will Bring

  • Engineering Degree/MBA degree or equivalent preferred
  • 7+ years of product management experience, and
  • 3+ years of experience in payments domain and platform payments processing. Deep knowledge of the payments landscape, embedded payments, white label solutions or PayFac models
  • Proven track record of creating "API-first" platforms and client-facing SDKs/UI components (e.g., drop-in frames for critical data collection).
  • Strategic mindset with the ability to plan execution towards a final "As-a-Service" vision, anticipating future regulatory and scalability needs for diverse tenants
  • Comfortable making the "tough calls" on trade-offs - balancing speed-to-market with the long-term health of a multi-tenant platform.
  • Ability to use data to formulate product perspectives, justify platform investments, and define success metrics for platform health and reliability.
  • Experienced in managing complex stakeholder relationships (Legal, Treasury, Risk, and Engineering) to achieve "win-win" outcomes for the platform and its tenants.
  • Balances technical depth and sharp eye for high-leverage outcomes; able to communicate what is possible and what will have the biggest impact on business


Additional Details

eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.

We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

items.descriptionHtml:
Atomicwork is on a mission to transform the digital workplace experience by uniting people, processes, and platforms through AI automation. Our team is building a modern service management platform that enables growing businesses to reduce operational complexity and drive business success.


We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to join our team and help us build our modern service management platform. We offer competitive pay to employees and practical benefits for their whole family.

If this sounds interesting to you, read on.


The opportunity for you

  • You’ll work across engineering, design, and other product teams to shape a scalable, secure, and flexible platform that can adapt to the evolving needs of our customers.
  • You’ll define the long-term vision for our core platform, ensuring it meets the needs of internal teams.
  • You will deeply understand the problems you are handling via research, data and customer conversations, and work on defining the problems and solutions with the team, with a focus on reliability and extensibility.
  • You will influence technical direction without having to write code and translating business goals into strong platform capabilities.


What we're looking for

  • 8+ years of product management experience working on platform, API, or developer-focused products in a SaaS environment.
  • Understand modern architecture patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven systems) and how they impact scalability, reliability, and security.
  • Can translate complex technical trade-offs into clear business decisions.
  • Good problem-solving skills, and the ability to switch between thinking creatively and technically
  • Strong communication skills, including the ability to document and present design ideas effectively.


Why we are different (culture)

As a part of Atomicwork, you can shape our company and business from idea to production. Our cultural values also set the bar high, helping us create a better workplace for everyone.

  • Agency: Be self-directed. Take initiative and solve problems creatively.
  • Taste: Hold a high bar. Sweat the details. Build with care and discernment.
  • Ownership: We demonstrate unwavering commitment to our mission and goals, taking full responsibility for triumphs and setbacks.
  • Mastery: We relentlessly pursue continuous self-improvement as individuals and teams, dedicating ourselves to constant learning and growth.
  • Impatience: We recognize that our world moves swiftly and is driven by an unyielding desire to progress with every endeavor.
  • Customer Obsession: We place our customers at the heart of everything we do, relentlessly seeking to understand their needs and exceed their expectations.


What we offer (compensation and benefits)

We are big on benefits that make sense to you and your family.

  • Fantastic team — The #1 reason people choose us (and stay).
  • Convenient offices — Well-located workspaces across Bengaluru and Palo Alto.
  • Comprehensive health insurance — Industry-leading coverage for you and your family.
  • Flexible allowances — Hassle-free reimbursements across a wide range of expenses.
  • Annual outings — Time to unwind, connect, and have fun as a team.
  • Office cabs — Seamless, stress-free commuting.
  • Fitness benefits — Gym memberships to support your well-being.
  • Snacks — Always stocked, with Friday meals on us.
  • Best-in-class tech — Top-tier tools and devices to keep you moving fast.
  • AI subscriptions — Access to leading AI tools to maximise productivity.


What next (applying for this role)

  • Click on the apply button to get started with your application.
  • Answer a few questions about yourself and your work.
  • Wait to hear from us about the next steps.


Do you have anything else to tell us? Email careers@atomicwork.com and let us know what’s on your mind.

items.descriptionHtml:
We have an excellent opportunity with travel leaders in Industry for Lead/Principal Product Manager with airline Solutions experience.


Looking for candidates specifically form Airline /OTA/Aviation domain experience.


Role: Lead/Principal Product Manager

Exp: 10+ Yrs

Location: Bangalore

Mode of Work: Hybrid(3 days from office)


Please find the below JD, if you are interested share your profile to swathid@scaleneworks.com.


JD:

Job Description Summary

Defining and driving an end‑to‑end Non‑Functional Requirements product strategy across Amadeus Airline Solutions, embedding security, performance, scalability, and resilience as core, value‑creating capabilities through close collaboration with product, architecture, engineering, and commercial teams


Job Description

Job Description

Summary of the role:

As a Principal Product Manager, you will own and drive the NFR product vision across the Airline Solutions portfolio, ensuring NFRs are designed as intrinsic, value‑creating product capabilities rather than after‑thoughts. You will work at the intersection of product strategy, business architecture, engineering, and commercial teams, influencing senior stakeholders and airline customers globally. This is a individual‑contributor role with broad influence, operating at portfolio and ecosystem level.


In this role you’ll:

The primary purpose of this role is to define, structure, and promote a coherent end‑to‑end NFR product strategy that strengthens the value of Airline Solutions, supports airline transformation programs, and enables scalable, secure, and resilient solutions aligned with Amadeus’ product life‑cycle and architecture principles.


Product Strategy & Vision

· Define and own the NFR product vision, roadmap, and portfolio blueprint across Airline Solutions.

· Ensure NFRs are embedded into the Product Life Cycle (PLC) as first‑class product capabilities.

· Translate airline business needs and market expectations into clear, value‑driven NFR capabilities.

Portfolio & Architecture Leadership

· Structure and manage the NFR scope in close collaboration with Epic Owners, Business Architects, and Engineering.

· Lead and contribute to business architecture studies on complex or unclear NFR topics arising from RFI/RFPs or strategic initiatives.

· Ensure consistency and alignment of NFRs across business domains, products, and solution layers.

· Identify common, reusable capabilities and dependencies, including alignment with transversal and travel capabilities.

Customer & Commercial Engagement

· Act as NFR Stream Leader during airline scoping phases.

· Lead and coordinate NFR contributions for RFI/RFP responses and customer proposals.

· Participate in customer workshops, pre‑sales engagements, and executive discussions to articulate Amadeus’ NFR value proposition.

· Provide clear inputs to commercial teams on NFR packaging, positioning, baselines, and options.

Cross‑Functional Collaboration & Influence

· Work closely with Product Management, Product Marketing, Engineering, Architecture, Services, and Commercial teams.

· Ensure alignment between business intent, functional design, and technical strategy.

· Facilitate decision‑making across multiple stakeholders on complex, transversal topics.

· Act as a thought leader and ambassador for NFR excellence within Amadeus.


Success Measures

· Quality, clarity, and impact of NFR strategy, blueprints, and studies.

· Consistent, high‑quality NFR contributions in RFI/RFPs and customer engagements.

· Positive feedback from airline customers, senior management, and engineering teams.

· Demonstrated impact of NFR design on overall solution value and competitiveness.

· Effective cross‑domain alignment and timely delivery of key artefacts.



About the ideal candidate:

  • 8–10+ years of experience in Product Management, Solution Management, or related roles.
  • Strong knowledge of the airline industry, airline IT ecosystems, and Airline Solutions.
  • Proven experience managing complex, transversal product capabilities end‑to‑end.
  • Solid understanding of Non‑Functional Requirements (security, scalability, performance, availability, reliability, etc.).
  • Experience working with platform and cloud‑based architectures.
  • Exposure to enterprise or business architecture is a strong advantage.
  • Experience with Agile / SAFe or similar frameworks.
  • University degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or Business (or equivalent experience).

items.descriptionHtml:
Product Manager – Supply Chain & Operations

What Makes This Role Exciting

Product Strategy & Ownership

  • Own the product vision and roadmap for end-to-end supply chain systems covering demand planning, procurement, production planning, warehousing, inventory, and logistics
  • Translate business goals such as freshness, fill rate, cost optimization, and SLA adherence into measurable product outcomes and KPIs
  • Identify opportunities for automation, digitization, and process optimization across supply chain operations
  • Drive scalable product solutions that improve operational efficiency and business performance

Execution & Delivery

  • Lead product discovery, prioritization, and execution across internal operational platforms
  • Create detailed PRDs, define user journeys, and collaborate closely with Engineering, Design, and Operations teams
  • Drive data-backed experimentation and continuous process improvements
  • Ensure timely delivery of high-impact features in a fast-paced environment

Demand, Inventory & Planning

  • Build products that enhance demand forecasting, replenishment planning, and inventory optimization
  • Partner with Data Science teams to develop forecasting models and decision-support systems
  • Enable real-time visibility into inventory, wastage, fulfillment, and operational capacity across multiple nodes
  • Improve inventory turns while maintaining service levels and operational efficiency

Operations & Reliability

  • Design systems that improve traceability, compliance, and quality control across supply chain workflows
  • Build operational dashboards, alerting systems, and exception management frameworks
  • Balance platform stability and operational reliability with rapid product iteration in a 24x7 environment
  • Improve process visibility and operational decision-making through scalable internal tools

Stakeholder Management

  • Act as the primary product stakeholder for Supply Chain and Operations leadership
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Finance, Category, Retail, and Business teams on cost, margins, and product availability
  • Drive roadmap prioritization by balancing operational requirements, customer impact, and technical scalability
  • Influence strategic product decisions across multiple business functions

What We’re Looking For

Experience

  • 6–10 years of Product Management experience in Supply Chain, Logistics, Manufacturing, Retail Operations, FMCG, or related domains
  • Hands-on experience building internal operational platforms or enterprise workflow products
  • Proven track record of delivering complex, scalable products in high-growth or high-volume environments

Skills & Capabilities

  • Strong analytical and structured problem-solving skills
  • Data-driven mindset with experience working on metrics, dashboards, operational KPIs, and forecasting models
  • Ability to thrive in ambiguous and evolving operational environments
  • Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and cross-functional collaboration skills
  • Strong execution ownership with a focus on operational impact

Good to Have

  • Experience in fresh food, cold chain, or perishable supply chain environments

items.descriptionHtml:
We are seeking a dynamic Lead Product Manager to join our team and work closely with our founders, financial analysts, engineers, and product managers. In this role, you'll drive the development of innovative features and solutions that support our clients' success. You will play a key role in defining product vision, gathering and prioritizing customer requirements, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver impactful products. Your ability to balance customer needs with business goals and lead product initiatives from concept to execution will be key to driving our platform's growth and success.


Responsibilities:

  • Develop and implement the product vision for a data platform designed to meet the needs of institutional investors.
  • Collaborate with clients to understand their data engineering and analytical requirements.
  • Lead the development of critical features from the initial requirements phase through to final delivery.
  • Work closely with Product Engineers and Data Engineers to ensure optimal performance, scalability, and reliability of the data platform.

Requirements:

  • 5+ years of experience in product management, ideally with a focus on data platforms, data engineering, or analytics products.
  • Education from IIT or IIM with an 8.0+ GPA (mandatory). MBA is a plus.
  • Strong proficiency in data technologies, workflows, and tools like SQL, Python, and data engineering concepts.
  • Proven experience managing data-centric product development with both internal teams and external client requirements.
  • Excellent communication, client-facing, and cross-functional collaboration skills.

items.descriptionHtml:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.

Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.

Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.

About EBay

Are you seeking a company that encourages passion, courage, and creativity? Join eBay, where you can be part of a dynamic team crafting the future of global commerce. At eBay, we empower millions of people to buy, sell, connect, and share worldwide. In eBay Payments, you will be an integral member of a growing organization that inspires passion, courage, and inventiveness - creating the future of global commerce and making an important, positive impact on millions of eBay sellers and shoppers around the world. If you are looking for a special place to take your Payments career to the next level, we want to talk with you.

Role Overview

eBay needs a skilled Sr. Product Manager to lead the advancement of our Seller Payments team. This position emphasizes finding opportunities and implementing strategies with measurable effects. The role calls for strategic insight to promote innovation and boost market efficiency globally. The successful candidate will have a demonstrated ability to develop global product strategies and work alongside regional business leaders, product teams, and domain experts. They focus on building core payment capabilities and tailoring features for local market success. Outstanding ability to build relationships and influence partners at all levels is essential.

Key Responsibilities

  • Strategic Leadership: Define and complete the product strategy and roadmap for Seller Payments, ensuring a strengths-based approach with a global perspective.
  • Agile Product Ownership: Serve as the Product Owner, providing clear product vision to the scrum team and co-leading the Agile process with engineering counterparts to reach roadmap achievements.
  • Prioritization & Planning: Establish product/backlog prioritization, balancing immediate deliverables with long-term technology investments.
  • Platform Development: Translate commerce requirements into versatile payment capabilities adaptable to various processors, types, and models.
  • AI Integration: Leverage AI to enhance product capabilities, improve decision-making processes, and optimize user experiences!
  • Evangelism & Collaboration: Act as a product evangelist, working closely with business partners and engineering leaders to align strategies.
  • Communication & Influence: Effectively communicate strategies and trade-offs to senior leaders and development teams, encouraging alignment and understanding.
  • Innovation & Improvement: Incorporate market trends and technological developments into effective improvements for Seller Payments.


Job Requirements

  • Expertise: Deep subject matter expertise in technologies and products crucial for launching payment solutions at scale globally.
  • Agile Experience: Proficient in agile development processes.
  • UX Focus: Experience in developing innovative product features with a strong emphasis on UX development.
  • AI Skills: Knowledge of AI technologies and their use in product management to foster innovation and improve efficiency.
  • Analytical Skills: Ability to ask insightful questions to build product perspectives and articulate requirements.
  • Balance & Impact: Skill in balancing technical possibilities with business impact, communicating effectively to drive significant outcomes.
  • Relationship Building: Proven ability to build strong internal and external relationships, actively listen, communicate clearly, and work toward mutually beneficial solutions.


Basic Qualifications

  • Engineering Degree or MBA degree or equivalent experience preferred
  • 5+ years of robust product management experience preferably in e-commerce/marketplaces
  • 3+ years of experience in payments domain, Deep understanding of payments domain would be preferred


Additional Details

eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.

items.descriptionHtml:
About the Company

You will take full ownership of the core financial engine of the Pice app - credit card payments and repayments, which are the backbone of our user acquisition and monetisation. You will work directly with Product and Senior Leadership, who will provide strategic business, consumer, and market direction while you focus on day-to-day execution of product work that drives business outcomes and sets a high bar for user experience.



About the Role

Skills & Qualifications Required

  • 3–7 years of product management experience in consumer-facing fintech or payments apps. (Specific Experience in credit card bill payments & UPI is a plus)
  • Proven ability in building and scaling mobile app features with high user adoption & ability to drive 0-1 product cycles.
  • Deep understanding of digital payments (UPI, Cards, Wallets), lending workflows and credit rails.
  • Solid product thinking with an ability to simplify financial complexity into intuitive flows.
  • Ability to deliver polished, intuitive experiences in financial/payment workflows.
  • Comfortable working with compliance, operations, credit/risk teams and backend-heavy stacks.
  • Experience in implementing and monitoring analytics (frontend & backend).
  • Proficiency in project management tools such as Jira, Asana, and Notion.
  • Excellent stakeholder relationship management (across tech, design, marketing, sales).
  • Must be a total Team Player. Non-negotiable.



Responsibilities

  • Drive the execution of critical payment and credit features inside the Pice app.
  • Build user journeys that are localised, scalable, and deeply intuitive for the Bharat audience (Tier 1 & 2).
  • Independently carry out the entire product cycle: idea generation, wireframe, sprint planning, tech dev, testing & release.
  • Drive integrations with Payment Gateways, Credit Partners and internal infrastructure.
  • Work with our internal credit & compliance partners to ensure fast, safe releases.
  • Own delivery velocity, feature quality, and post-launch monitoring.
  • Work closely with Product Marketing and design well-thought-out GTM/feature releases for users.
  • Collaborate with Product Leadership on new feature idea/discovery, implementation & integration with the overall app architecture.



Interview Process

  • Round 1: CV/Resume Shortlisting
  • Round 2: Interview with Product & Business Leadership
  • Round 3: Interview with Founders

items.descriptionHtml:
About the Role:


We are looking for an experienced Senior Product Manager to own product development for Neo’s HNI wealth management business.


This role is for someone who can go deep into ambiguous problems, break them into clear product requirements, work closely with engineering and design, and drive execution from idea to adoption.


The ideal candidate has built 0→1 products, worked on products at scale, is strong in user behavior and UX thinking, understands technical trade-offs, and is hands-on with AI tools and automation.


Responsibilities:


● Own product strategy, roadmap, and execution for HNI-focused products.

● Deeply understand banker, client, operations, and investment workflows.

● Break complex business problems into clear product requirements, flows, and milestones.

● Work closely with engineering to identify blockers, clarify trade-offs, and unblock execution.

● Collaborate with designers to create intuitive, high-trust user experiences for bankers and HNI clients.

● Use data, user research, and behavioral insights to improve adoption, conversion, engagement, and efficiency.

● Build products across areas such as client onboarding, portfolio review, banker productivity, client engagement, reporting, and workflow automation.

● Stay hands-on with AI tools and identify opportunities to use AI, automation, agentic workflows, LLM orchestration, and RAG.

● Communicate clearly with leadership, business, compliance, design, engineering, and operations teams.

● Ensure products are shipped, adopted, measured, and continuously improved.


Requirements:


● 5+ years of product management experience.

● Strong problem-solving ability with a structured, first-principles approach.

● Strong execution mindset and ability to drive cross-functional teams.

● Good understanding of user behavior, UX, and product design.

● Ability to understand technical constraints and communicate them clearly to stakeholders.

● Hands-on experience using AI tools for research, analysis, documentation, prototyping, or workflow automation.

● Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

● Ability to work with data, funnels, dashboards, and product analytics.

items.descriptionHtml:
About Josys

Josys is a fast-growing B2B SaaS startup on a mission to simplify and automate IT operations for the modern workplace. Backed by $218M in Series A & B funding and a successful launch in Japan, we’re expanding globally with a footprint in 9 countries and a cutting-edge tech hub in Bengaluru, India.


Spun off from RAKSUL (a Forbes Startup of the Year), Josys offers an all-in-one platform for SaaS and device management, giving IT teams 360° visibility and powerful automation—from employee onboarding to offboarding.

With remote work and SaaS sprawl on the rise, Josys helps businesses cut IT costs by up to 75%, boost security, and stay compliant—all while making IT teams super efficient.

Position Overview:


We are looking for an experienced Senior Product Manager to join Josys and help shape the future of AI-Powered SaaS management.

As a Senior Product Manager at Josys, you’ll play a critical role in building a user-centric B2B SaaS product that streamlines IT SaaS operations, drives automation, and delivers tangible value to enterprises. You’ll own key product areas, collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, and lead initiatives from discovery to launch. This is a high-impact role where you’ll influence product strategy, prioritize what matters most, and ensure we ship solutions that scale.


Location: Bengaluru, India (Hybrid)


As a Senior Product Manager at Josys, you will:

  • Understand market trends & user needs to identify meaningful customer pain points, and translate them into intuitive, scalable product solutions.
  • Break down complex problem statements into actionable, incremental product delivery.
  • Own quarterly planning and roadmap creation for your product domain, setting clear short-term and long-term objectives.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with engineering, design, marketing, sales, and customer success teams to define product requirements and drive end-to-end execution.
  • Conduct customer interviews and analyze feedback to uncover insights that inform product decisions, enhancements, and new feature development.
  • Lead User Acceptance Testing (UAT) to validate features and ensure the product meets user expectations before launch.
  • Track adoption and usage of delivered features, gather user feedback and data insights, and drive iterative improvements to maximize product impact.


What You'll Bring :

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, or related field. MBA is a plus.
  • 5+ years of product management experience, particularly in the B2B SaaS domain.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills, with a keen eye for detail.
  • Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and manage time effectively in a fast-paced environment.
  • Proven ability to lead and align cross-functional teams to drive shared product success
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.
  • Proactive and self-motivated, with a continuous learning mindset and a passion for keeping up with emerging technologies and industry trends.

About Qure.AI


Qure.AI is an equal opportunity employer. Qure.ai is a leading Healthcare Artificial Intelligence (AI) company disrupting the ‘status quo’ by enhancing diagnostic imaging and improving health outcomes with the assistance of machine-supported tools.

Qure.ai taps deep learning technology to provide automated interpretation of radiology exams like X-rays and CT scans for time- and resource-strapped medical imaging professionals, enabling faster diagnosis and speed to treatment. Qure.ai aims to democratize healthcare by improving accuracy, accessibility, and affordability for patients worldwide. Founded in 2016, we apply the latest AI research to healthcare problems and develop innovative solutions that are revolutionizing the way patients are diagnosed and treated. Our solutions have impacted more than 38 million lives, deployed in 106+ countries and 4800+ sites globally. Qure.ai is an equal opportunity employer.


About the job

Job Title: Senior Product Manager

Department: Products

Location: Bangalore

Years of Experience: 5 - 10 years

Employment Type: Full time


Key Relationships

Vice President of Products


About the Role

We are seeking a passionate and results-driven Product Leader in Operations and Sales to lead the expansion of our AI-powered lung cancer solution to the US market. This is a mid- senior, and hands-on role in a highly execution-focused environment, where product decisions directly impact revenue conversion, throughput, cost efficiency, and the reliability of on-ground operations.

You will work across multiple U.S. healthcare clients and channel/vendor partnerships, collaborating closely with end users, system administrators, and IT teams to translate complex workflows and gaps into tailored solutions. You will own prompt requirements and engineering to ensure agentic workflows function effectively, take accountability for NPS by driving rigorous quality assurance sign-offs, and define and monitor analytics as needed.

While the role offers strong influence on product roadmap and release planning, it also demands depth stepping in to guide teams on workflows, system design, and PRDs, especially in situations involving high ambiguity, scale, or risk.


Roles & Responsibilities

  • Solution Architecture & Scoping: Own end-to-end outcomes across the lead-to-install lifecycle by scoping client requirements, integration mapping, and implementation readiness to translate complex clinical workflows into AI-enabled solutions
  • Agentic AI Ownership: Lead the prompt engineering and logic design for AI agents, ensuring high-performance automated workflows function reliably within client-specific environments, with continuous optimization based on performance data and clinical accuracy
  • Product Strategy and Oversight: Influence product roadmaps and release planning by validating client insights, identifying bottlenecks and failure points, and balancing short-term execution with long-term scalability and resilience; act as the bridge between frontline feedback and internal product teams
  • Client Success & NPS: Manage multi-channel partnerships and diverse healthcare portfolios to ensure clinical alignment, driving top-tier NPS within the first 3 months of site onboarding
  • Quality Governance: Serve as the final authority for last-mile client QA, ensuring all solutions meet rigorous healthcare industry standards and specific user needs.
  • Cross-Functional Leadership: Drive alignment and manage trade-off decisions across Business Development, Operations, and Engineering to ensure project velocity and technical integrity
  • Performance Optimization: Monitor and analyze system performance data to provide actionable insights, ensuring agentic workflows are continuously optimized for efficiency and clinical accuracy
  • Sales & Growth Enablement: Architect high-impact product collaterals. including demos, pitch-decks, battlecards one-pagers to equip BD and CS teams with the tools needed to close deals
  • Customer Advocacy: Act as the "Voice of the Customer," leveraging a deep understanding of payer and end-user needs to ensure feedback is effectively integrated into the product lifecycle


Skills & Expertise

  • Education: Bachelor's degree in Technology, Business, Healthcare, or a related field.
  • Experience: 5+ years of product management experience in AI, B2B SaaS, or Healthcare companies. Experience with AI/ML-powered products is a strong plus
  • Entrepreneurial Spirit: A proactive and entrepreneurial mindset with a strong drive to succeed in a dynamic and fast-paced environment.
  • Communication & Presentation: Excellent written and verbal communication, strong presentation and public speaking skills.
  • Entrepreneurial Spirit: A proactive and entrepreneurial mindset with a strong drive to succeed in a dynamic and fast-paced environment.


Please note Note:

  • This role requires frequent travel to the US.
  • Having a US visa is a plus, and prior travel to the UK or European countries is also an advantage.


At Qure.ai

You will be part of a rapidly growing team, with an opportunity to work alongside the best minds in the industry. The chance to work on real problems that impact lives globally. Competitive compensation and rewards, opportunity to lead, grow, and define how design shapes healthcare experiences globally.


Inclusive workspace

Applicant Privacy Policy

By submitting your information and application, you confirm that you have read and agree to the Applicant Privacy Policy:

https://www.qure.ai/applicant-privacy-policy

items.descriptionHtml:
About Bright Money

Bright Money is a Consumer FinTech for middle-income consumers. It brings together SaaS products to help consumers build credit, manage their debt, and get access to suitable loans and credit cards. These are the major financial needs of middle-income consumers living paycheck-to-paycheck.


Bright is a profitable, India-built, U.S.-market Consumer FinTech, with revenue trajectory to $100M in the coming 12 months. The business model combines subscription revenue with financial service marketplace revenues for loans, credit cards, and credit-building tools.


Bright has launched an AI money assistant and AI agentic UX flows for access to credit. It will be the first AI-driven Consumer FinTech for middle-income consumers.


The Bright product suite has potential for global markets beyond the US. The market size is 100M global middle-income consumers, with a revenue pool opportunity of $15B.


Bright is backed by 3 top global venture capital firms, and leading tech angel investors in the USA, Europe, and Asia.


Bright was founded in 2019 by a founding team from McKinsey’s Banking Practice (Petko Plachkov and Avi Patchava) and InMobi Data Scientists (Varun Modi and Avi Patchava). The core team is ex-InMobi Engineering and Data Sciences, recently joined by senior leaders from McKinsey.


Bright is a data-driven, fast-paced execution culture that will build a Global Consumer FinTech, all functions operating from India.


What is the team & role about?


We are hiring a Platform Product Manager - Banking Platform to own and scale core banking and money-movement platform capabilities that power multiple financial products. This role focuses on bank account lifecycles, payment rails, partner integrations, and regulatory-ready infrastructure, enabling product teams to build reliably and at scale.


This is a senior product leadership role combining deep platform ownership, people management, executive-level communication, and structured problem-solving. You will operate as a trusted partner to Engineering leadership and CXOs, translating complex technical and regulatory challenges into clear decisions and execution plans.


What will you do?


Product Ownership & Strategy

  • Own the vision, strategy, and roadmap for one or more Banking Services platform domains (e.g., account onboarding, verification, bank transfers, settlements).
  • Define clear product goals, success metrics, and trade-offs across reliability, compliance, and feature delivery.
  • Manage the full product lifecycle—from discovery and requirements to launch, adoption, and optimization.


Banking Infrastructure & Money Movement

  • Drive platform capabilities supporting bank account lifecycles and money movement (e.g., ACH equivalents, local bank transfers, instant payments, reconciliation workflows).
  • Lead integrations with banking partners, payment processors, and financial infrastructure providers.
  • Ensure strong handling of edge cases such as failures, retries, reversals, exceptions, and settlements.


Compliance, Risk & Resilience

  • Translate regulatory, scheme, and partner constraints into clear platform requirements (e.g., auditability, controls, data retention, reporting).
  • Partner with Risk, Compliance, Legal, and Operations to ensure controls are embedded into platform design.
  • Drive platform resilience designing for partner outages, degraded modes, and recovery scenarios.


Execution & Delivery

  • Write high-quality PRDs and platform requirements that engineers can execute against with clarity.
  • Prioritize across run-the-business work, platform improvements, and new enablement initiatives.
  • Lead cross-functional execution across multiple engineering teams.


Stakeholder & Internal Customer Experience

  • Act as the product owner for internal consumers of the platform (Product, Engineering, Ops).
  • Improve developer and internal product team experience through clear APIs, documentation, and self-serve capabilities.
  • Communicate progress, risks, and decisions clearly to senior leadership.


Ideal candidate profile looks like?

Experience & Background:

  • 5 - 12 years of Product Management experience, with strong exposure to backend, platform, or infrastructure products.
  • Prior people management experience, including mentoring or managing Product Managers
  • Exposure to high-scale, high-availability systems with strict SLAs.
  • Strong understanding of APIs, distributed systems, data platforms, and cloud architectures.
  • Strong systems thinking and technical fluency, comfortable working deeply with engineers on architecture and trade-offs.


Mindset & Operating DNA:

  • Experience defining and using KPIs/OKRs to drive prioritization and outcomes.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Proven ability to manage complex dependencies and cross-functional stakeholders.


Non-Fit Profiles:

  • Domain experts without Platform Ownership experience.
  • PMs from Low-Scale or Low-Reliability Environments.
  • Product Managers without deep technical depth.
  • Pure UI/ UX-First Product Managers.
  • Program Manager or Project Managers.


Preferred Skills:

  • Experience building internal platforms or developer-facing products.
  • Exposure to global-scale, high-availability systems.
  • Experience in fintech, banking, payments, or other regulated systems.


What Bright Offers?

  • A profitable sub-unicorn with U.S. market scale – liquidity potential at $1–3B valuations.
  • Real ownership: You run your own BU with an independent P&L.
  • Wealth creation: Equity at an inflection stage of scale.
  • A data-driven, high-cadence culture that combines consulting rigor with FinTech speed.


Location flexibility: 5 days from the Bangalore office in a week
